analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$78,100 on Apr 24 morning UTC. Range today: $77,500-$78,500. Volatility cooling, derivatives momentum fading. BTC-USD near-perfect inverse correlation at 4-year extreme (dollar weakness supports BTC long-term).
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 12 (Extreme Fear) — deeply pessimistic retail sentiment, contrarian bullish signal
RSI: 65.26 — neutral, not overbought. 20 bullish vs 12 bearish technical indicators
BTC price range today: $77,500-$78,500 — tight compression, imminent volatility expansion risk
Long/short ratio: 50.68% — near-even split, market undecided on next direction
Derivatives: negative/cooling funding rates, cautious leverage, traders unwinding positions
Spot BTC ETF: 5 consecutive days of inflows; $245M by mid-April; $238M on Apr 21 alone
Strategy (Saylor) bought 34,164 BTC ($2.54B) on Apr 20 — largest acquisition since late 2024
BTC-USD inverse correlation near 4-year extreme (Apr 24) — dollar weakness historically bullish for BTC
Goldman Sachs filed for new Bitcoin ETF (regulatory tailwinds long-term bullish)
Morgan Stanley filed for spot Bitcoin ETF in Jan 2026
Altcoin season index: 39/100 — BTC dominance preferred over altcoins
KelpDAO $292M exploit hurt DeFi TVL (-$14B to 1-year low) — altcoin sentiment fragile, BTC relatively unaffected
CPI Mar 2026: 3.3% YoY (up from 2.4% Feb) — elevated inflation, no rate cuts expected near-term
CME monthly BTC+ETH options expiry today (Apr 24 16:00 UTC) — key volatility event
Bitcoin Core v31.0 upgrade (Apr 2026) — redesigned mempool, mandatory Tor/I2P privacy
analysis.macro_backdrop: Fed funds 3.50-3.75% (on hold). FOMC Apr 28-29 — ~99% probability of hold (no cut/hike). CPI 3.3% limits dovish pivot. GDP Q1 advance + PCE due Apr 30 — major risk event beyond 24h window. Kevin Warsh (Fed chair nominee) stressed Fed independence from Trump rate-cut pressure. Geopolitical: Iran/Hormuz tensions persist, oil prices elevated. Iran ceasefire stalling — risk-off pressure on macro assets. Big Tech earnings (MSFT/Apple) clustered Apr 29-30.
analysis.technical_note: BTC $78,100 — consolidating below $80K resistance. Short-term forecast: $75,329-$79,755 range next 24h. 2-day forecast: +2.89% to ~$80,035. CME options expiry at 16:00 UTC today is primary near-term catalyst. Confidence 0.45 — below 0.50 bot threshold — macro filter INACTIVE this cycle.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral: Institutional demand (ETF inflows, Saylor $2.54B buy, Goldman/Morgan Stanley filings, BTC-USD inverse at 4yr extreme) offset by Fear & Greed 12 (Extreme Fear), near-even long/short ratio, cooling derivatives momentum, CPI 3.3% (no dovish pivot), CME expiry volatility risk, FOMC uncertainty Apr 28-29. BTC trading in narrow range — no clear directional conviction despite structural bullish tailwinds.
